title: Attention Management

content.statement: Spend most of your time on the two or three areas with the highest payoff.
content.question: What two or three areas deserve 80% of your attention right now?
content.equation: Founder Output = Attention Quality × Leverage × Time Allocation
content.mental_model: Magnifying glass, focused attention creates heat.
content.business_lens: Identify the few activities that drive revenue, growth, team quality, or enterprise value and protect time for them.
content.life_lens: Give your best energy to the few things that truly shape the life you want.
sop: Identify the two or three with the highest payoff
Block 80% of deep work around those areas
Reduce, delegate, or delay the rest
content.explainer: Attention Management is about choosing where your mind goes, not just managing your calendar. Founders often spend too much time reacting to whatever is loudest. But the loudest task is not always the highest-payoff task. When your attention concentrates, momentum builds.
questions: What two or three areas deserve 80% of your attention right now?
What's the loudest task on your plate this week that isn't actually the highest-payoff one?
If you only worked 10 hours next week, which activities would you protect first?

definitions.def: The multiplier effect of an action, doing something once that creates value far beyond the time or effort it took.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Attention Quality 7, Leverage 9, Time Allocation 6. Score = 7 times 9 times 6 = 378 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means your hours are producing outsized results. Lower means you're busy without moving the business forward.
content.closing_statement: Protect your attention like it's capital.

caption:
Principle Nº 261: Attention Management

Spend most of your time on the two or three areas with the highest payoff. Attention Management is about choosing where your mind goes, not just managing your calendar.

Ask yourself: What two or three areas deserve 80% of your attention right now?
